title: With Upavāṇa
text:
## 1. Mountains

At one time the venerables Upavāṇa and Sāriputta were staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery. Then in the late afternoon, Venerable Sāriputta came out of retreat, went to Venerable Upavāṇa and exchanged greetings with him. When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Upavāṇa:

“Reverend Upavāṇa, can a mendicant know by investigating inside themselves that the seven awakening factors are properly implemented so that they lead to living at ease?” “They can, Reverend Sāriputta.

As a mendicant rouses up the awakening factor of mindfulness, they understand: ‘My mind is well freed. I’ve eradicated dullness and drowsiness, and eliminated restlessness and remorse. My energy is roused up, and my mind is sharply focused, not sluggish.’ … As they rouse up the awakening factor of equanimity, they understand: ‘My mind is well freed. I’ve eradicated dullness and drowsiness, and eliminated restlessness and remorse. My energy is roused up, and my mind is sharply focused, not sluggish.’ That’s how a mendicant can know by investigating inside themselves that the seven awakening factors are properly implemented so that they lead to living at ease.”
